user_query:
We’re a US Amazon seller of small kitchen electronics (SKUs include an electric kettle and a countertop milk frother). We want to expand to the UK, Germany, France, Canada, and Japan in the next 90 days. Please read all context files under input/ and produce three deliverables:

1) A structured expansion plan at output/expansion_plan.json using this exact 5-part format: (1) Market Entry Analysis (opportunity size, competition, compliance overview) per market, (2) VAT Registration Roadmap (step-by-step) per market, (3) Localization Checklist (listing, packaging, compliance) per market, (4) Pricing Recommendation (local currency pricing strategy with VAT/fees/tax considerations), (5) Launch Sequence (prioritized order with rationale and inventory strategy suggestion: EFN vs Pan-EU vs multi-country inventory).

2) A tracking and measurement plan at output/tracking_plan.md that includes: GA4-style event definitions for the full e-commerce funnel (product_viewed, add_to_cart, checkout_started, purchase_completed at minimum), standard event properties, UTM naming conventions, validation checklist, and privacy/consent guidance for EU/UK/CA and Japan (consent mode, no PII in event parameters). Include a brief section on how we’ll attribute revenue by market and SKU.

3) A pricing file at output/pricing.csv with columns: sku, market, local_currency, recommended_price, includes_vat (true/false), pricing_notes. For EU/UK rows, includes_vat must be true; for CA and JP, set this based on local norms you recommend (and explain in pricing_notes). Use appropriate local charm pricing conventions (e.g., .99 or .95 endings, and for Japan use typical price endings). Provide at least one row per SKU per market listed in input/target_markets.csv.

Please reflect the details of our business, products, competitor pricing, and team capacity when making recommendations. Files to read are in input/.
